title: toxicodendron macrocarpum
intro: Toxicodendron macrocarpum, also known as the poison oak or eastern poison oak, is a species of poisonous plant that belongs to the Anacardiaceae family. Native to eastern North America, it is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional folk medicine, where it has been used to treat various health conditions due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipruritic properties. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce fever, treat skin irritations and allergies, and act as an anti-oxidant, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of Toxicodendron macrocarpum can be found in the form of tinctures, salves, and infusions, which are often used to treat conditions such as eczema, acne, and dermatitis.
